content.explanation:
🧾 What is Form Validation?

When users fill in forms, you often need to make sure they enter correct and complete information.

This is called form validation.

🌐 Why Validation Matters

  • Prevents empty submissions
  • Ensures correct data format
  • Improves security and user experience

⚡ Real-Life Example

If someone tries to sign up without an email, the system should stop them and show an error.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Basic Validation Attributes

<input type="text" required>
<input type="email" required>
<input type="password" minlength="6" required>

📌 Key Attributes

  • required → field must not be empty
  • minlength → minimum number of characters
  • type="email" → checks email format

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Validation happens before data is submitted to a server

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Sign Up Form

Create your account below:

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Secure your form

1. Add 'required' to both inputs
2. Add minlength="6" to the password field
3. Make sure email uses type="email"

🔥 This makes your form behave like a real app
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example:
<input required>
<input minlength="6">

These attributes go inside input tags

quiz_engine.question: What does the 'required' attribute do in an HTML form?
options: It makes the field mandatory before submission
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The required attribute ensures that users cannot submit a form without filling in that field.

takeaways: Validation checks user input before submission
Validation improves security and user experience

cheat_sheet.required: Field must be filled before submit
cheat_sheet.validation: Rules that check user input correctness
